slides.title: Mastering Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn: Chapter 3
Introduction to Scikit-Learn
slides.content:
Scikit-Learn is a powerful, user-friendly Python library for machine learning. It provides simple and efficient tools for data mining and analysis.

Key Features:
- Versatility
- Simple and efficient tools for data mining and data analysis
- Built on NumPy, SciPy, and matplotlib

slides.title: Understanding Data Preprocessing
slides.content:
What is data preprocessing?
Data preprocessing is a technique used to convert raw data into a clean data set. It is crucial in machine learning.

Why is it important?
- Enhances feature quality
- Improves model effectiveness

visual_assets.text: "Preprocessing data is not just a step but a pathway to successful model training." - Anonymous
visual_assets.caption: Essence of Data Preprocessing

slides.title: Standardization vs. Normalization
slides.content:
Standardization: Scales the data to have a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1.

Normalization: Scales data to a range of [0, 1] or [-1, 1].

When to use?
Standardization is preferred when the distribution is Gaussian.
Normalization is used when data does not assume any distribution.

visual_assets.code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_data = scaler.fit_transform(data)
visual_assets.caption: Code Example: Standardization

slides.title: Handling Missing Data
slides.content:
Strategies to handle missing data:
1. Removal:
   - Remove rows or columns with missing values
2. Imputation:
   - Fill missing values using specific strategies (mean, median, mode)
3. Use prediction algorithms to estimate missing values

slides.title: Categorical Data Encoding
slides.content:
Why encode categorical data?
- Machine learning algorithms require numerical input

Types of encoding:
1. Label Encoding
2. One-Hot Encoding

visual_assets.code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
ohe = OneHotEncoder()
data_encoded = ohe.fit_transform(data)
visual_assets.caption: Code Example: One-Hot Encoding

slides.title: Conclusion & Best Practices
slides.content:
1. Ensure data quality before model training
2. Choose appropriate preprocessing techniques
3. Regularly test with validation data
4. Stay updated with Scikit-Learn updates
